purpose: User-invocable forked-context Opus skill (`!p`) — lightweight parallel dispatch without full dashboard tracking. The master plans + dispatches workers with rich prompts, but skips progress files, master_state.json, and metrics.json. Decision matrix vs !p_track: <3 tasks/single wave/focused -> !p; 3+ tasks OR >1 wave OR live monitoring -> !p_track. Master writes only initialization.json (plan snapshot) + bookend logs.json entries. Workers write nothing to dashboard — coordination via conversation context only. Auto-escalation rule: if plan has 3+ agents or >1 wave, master SHOULD recommend !p_track. Defers to _commands/Synapse/p.md, agent/instructions/tracker_master_instructions.md, and {project_root}/CLAUDE.md.
gotchas: No worker progress files = no live tracking = blind during execution
No master_state.json = no compaction recovery checkpoint
No metrics.json = no parallel efficiency calculations post-swarm
Auto-escalation is advisory — user can override and stay in !p mode
Workers use a different TEMPLATE_VERSION (p_v2) than !p_track (p_track_v2) — checked by validate-worker-prompt.sh with reduced required-section set
patterns: Lightweight dispatch: plan + bookend logs only
